title: Financial statement report
description: Data contract for the financial-statement format: a formal, ruled, hierarchical accounting statement (P&L / balance sheet / cash flow / ledger) rendered as a centred print-grade sheet with a statement masthead, ruled line-item rows (indented hierarchy, subtotal/total rules), right-aligned tabular-number period columns, and a variance column. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema and is the canonical example.

meta.description: Shared report metadata block (identical across all formats). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

org.description: Reporting entity / organisation. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Independent Research) when brand-neutral.

project.description: Project or reporting unit. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Open Research) when brand-neutral.

reportType.description: Machine label for the deliverable kind.

title.description: Statement title shown as the largest type on the page (the .fs-title).
examples: Consolidated Statement of Profit or Loss

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst under the title.

date.description: Issue / generation date (ISO yyyy-mm-dd). Rendered in footer + signoff.

author.description: Preparer / author. Use a real or neutral value (e.g. Research Desk) when brand-neutral.

theme.description: Initial brand theme applied as <html data-theme>. Default for this format is "blue".

statementType.description: Which standard accounting statement this is. Drives title vocabulary and the common-size base (revenue for pl; total assets for balance; net cash for cashflow).

currency.description: Currency, unit, basis of preparation, and statement status shown in the masthead caption row.

unit.description: Scale the figures are expressed in (shown as "Figures in …").

basis.description: Basis of preparation note for the caption.

status.description: Statement status badge: draft -> hold colour; final/audited -> ok colour.

periods.description: Ordered reporting-period columns (newest first by convention). Each becomes a right-aligned tabular-number column with a per-period show/hide toggle.

key.description: Stable period key referenced by every line's values map and by variance.

lines.description: The statement rows, in display order. Sections, line items, subtotals, and totals together form the ruled ledger; the renderer applies ruling and indentation from kind/level.

id.description: Unique row id (used for parent links, the common-size base, and note anchors).

label.description: Account / line description shown in the sticky first column.

level.description: Indent depth 0..n; drives left padding of the label. Headers are usually level 0; nested items 1..n.

kind.description: Row role controlling ruling and behaviour: header = section band (no figures, collapses its group); item = ordinary line item; subtotal = rule above + semibold; total = heavy rule above and double underline below.

values.description: Period-keyed figures: { periodKey -> number }. Negative numbers are costs/outflows; the renderer can parenthesise + colour them. Omit for header rows.

parent.description: Optional id of the section-header row this line belongs to; lets that header collapse its children while keeping the subtotal visible.

note.description: Optional footnote id (matches a footnotes[].id); rendered as a superscript marker on the label.

emphasis.description: If true, render the row bold even when it is an ordinary item (e.g. a headline KPI line).

variance.description: Optional variance column comparing two periods. The column can be toggled on/off and switched between absolute and percentage.

basePeriod.description: Period key used as the baseline (the 'from' period).

comparePeriod.description: Period key compared against the baseline (the 'to' period); variance = compare - base.

show.description: Whether the variance column is visible initially.

commonSize.description: Optional common-size configuration: the base line every figure is divided by when the common-size toggle is on (e.g. total revenue for a P&L, total assets for a balance sheet).

baseLineId.description: id of the line used as the 100% base.

label.description: Human label for the base (shown in the common-size control).

footnotes.description: Optional numbered notes referenced by line items via lines[].note.

id.description: Note id referenced by a line's note field.

signoff.description: Optional sign-off block giving the signed-document feel (Prepared by / Reviewed by / Date).

note.description: Optional closing note, e.g. 'Subject to audit.'

meta.title: Consolidated Statement of Profit or Loss
meta.subtitle: For the financial years ended 31 March
meta.author: Research Desk

footnotes.text: Subscription revenue is recognised rateably over the contract term (ASC 606 / IFRS 15).
